prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: The Neighbor Who Babysat Too Much
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Divorce Gothic
Tagline: Every town has a basement.
Theme: Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.
Omen: a lake bell ringing without wind
Relationship dynamic: career vs family guilt
Props: snow globe, scarf with impossible timing, ceramic owl

Cast:
- Brooke Cross | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=bakery owner | threat_aura=0.34 | space=annex room described as retreat supplies | secrets=once left town without explaining why; buried one traumatic detail under work for years
- Jenna Marlowe | archetype=decorative_husband | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=financial advisor | threat_aura=0.26 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=was in the wrong county on the wrong night; moved money during a family emergency
- Noah Pike | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=real estate agent | threat_aura=0.5 | space=unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files | secrets=knows about a half sibling no one asked for
- Elias Vale | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=gallery owner | threat_aura=0.83 | space=dock office with a desk nobody fully claims | secrets=knows why the old orchard chapel was closed; inherited documents she should have burned
- Cole Mercer | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=widowed boat mechanic | threat_aura=0.55 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=once repaired a lock that should not have existed; knows more about the vineyard fire than he admits
- Daniel Vale | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=innkeeper | threat_aura=0.36 | space=old storm shelter from before the divorce | secrets=keeps screenshots like religious texts
- Claire Hale | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Wyatt Vale | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Jenna Marlowe is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Brooke Cross says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Blackthorn Lake.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Jenna?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Claire. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Brooke. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The snow globe is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Elias."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."

Write an expansion JSON object with exactly these keys:
- expanded_synopsis: string, 180-320 words
- scene_paragraphs: array of 6 strings, each 90-180 words, representing juicy HallTime scene expansion
- dialogue_lines: array of 14 strings, each almost-human HallTime dialogue
